name: Upgrade vitest to >=3.2.6 — arbitrary file read when UI server running (CVE-2026-47429)
description: [FCR 2026-06-21 · L13-03] vitest@3.2.4 (pulled via @nx/react→@nx/vite) allows arbitrary file read/execute via /__vitest_api__ when the UI dev server runs (CVE-2026-47429), fixed >=3.2.6. Root cause: pnpm-lock resolves vitest to 3.2.4 with no override pinning it to the safe version. Fix: add a pnpm override `"vitest": ">=3.2.6"` in the root package.json overrides block.
acceptanceCriteria: Root cause: vitest@3.2.4 resolved transitively via @nx/react→@nx/vite; CVE-2026-47429 allows arbitrary file read/execute via /__vitest_api__ when the UI dev server is running. Behavioral variants: (a) UI dev server running — /__vitest_api__ endpoint exposed, exploitable by any network-adjacent attacker; (b) CI/headless run without UI server — endpoint not exposed, exploit not reachable. Both variants are remediated by upgrading the package. Regression verification: after adding pnpm override `"vitest": ">=3.2.6"` and running `pnpm install`, confirm `pnpm ls vitest` reports no version below 3.2.6. Run `pnpm exec nx run-many -t test` — all tests must remain green. Fix is done when: (1) pnpm-lock.yaml records vitest >=3.2.6 only, (2) `pnpm audit` no longer flags CVE-2026-47429, (3) full test suite stays green.
riskImpact: vitest@3.2.4 (CVE-2026-47429) allows arbitrary file read and potential remote code execution via the /__vitest_api__ endpoint when the Vitest UI dev server is running. Attacker with network access to the dev server can exfiltrate source files or execute arbitrary files. Blast radius: development/CI environments where the UI server runs; not the production runtime, but sensitive source and secrets on developer machines are at risk.
